news.title: Quarterly Research Impact Report Q4 2025
news.content:
Our Q4 2025 research impact report shows a 45% increase in article downloads and a 30% increase in citation rates across all VA-RA journals. The Journal of Biomedical Informatics continues to lead with the highest impact factor of 6.3.

Notable highlights:
- Over 1,200 articles published in 2025
- 500,000+ article downloads
- Researchers from 35+ countries
- 15% increase in journal submissions
news.excerpt: Q4 2025 shows record growth with 45% increase in downloads across all journals.
news.author: Analytics Team

news.title: New Journal Indexing Partnership with Major Academic Databases
news.content:
VA-RA has established new partnerships with several leading academic databases to ensure wider visibility and accessibility of research published in our journals. All VA-RA journals will now be indexed in Scopus, Web of Science, and Google Scholar.

This milestone represents a significant step forward in our mission to democratize access to high-quality academic content and provide researchers with maximum visibility for their work.
news.excerpt: VA-RA journals are now indexed in Scopus, Web of Science, and Google Scholar.
news.author: VA-RA Admin

news.title: Call for Papers: International Conference on Machine Learning 2026
news.content:
VA-RA is proud to announce the Call for Papers for the International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML 2026). We invite researchers and practitioners to submit original research contributions on all aspects of machine learning.

Topics of interest include but are not limited to:
- Deep Learning architectures and training methods
- Reinforcement Learning
- Natural Language Processing
- Computer Vision
- Fairness, Accountability, and Transparency in AI
- Quantum Machine Learning

Important Dates:
- Submission Deadline: April 15, 2026
- Notification of Acceptance: June 1, 2026
- Camera-Ready Deadline: July 1, 2026
- Conference Dates: August 15-18, 2026
news.excerpt: Submit your research to ICML 2026 — deadline April 15, 2026.
news.author: Conference Committee

news.title: VA-RA Launches New AI-Powered Research Tools Suite
news.content:
We are excited to announce the launch of our new suite of AI-powered research tools designed specifically for academic researchers. The tools include a grammar checker optimized for scientific writing, a citation generator supporting multiple formats, and a research analytics dashboard.

These tools leverage the latest advances in natural language processing and machine learning to help researchers improve the quality of their manuscripts, maintain proper citation practices, and gain insights into research trends within their fields.

More tools including a plagiarism detector, AI writing assistant, and reference manager are currently in development and will be released in the coming months.
news.excerpt: VA-RA introduces a comprehensive suite of AI-powered tools to streamline the research workflow for academics worldwide.
news.author: VA-RA Editorial Team
